cards.front: How to sort numbers according to tens place using comparator in Java?
cards.back: Use the Comparator interface and its compare method to compare the tens place of each number.

cards.front: What is the purpose of using Comparator in sorting numbers?
cards.back: It allows us to compare and sort numbers based on specific criteria, in this case, the tens place.

cards.front: How to compare two numbers based on their tens place using Comparator?
cards.back: Use the compare method of the Comparator interface, passing the two numbers as arguments, and compare their tens place.

cards.front: When to use a custom Comparator instead of a predefined one?
cards.back: When you need to sort numbers in a specific way that doesn't match the predefined sorting order.

cards.front: Can we use Comparator with primitive types in Java?
cards.back: No, Comparator can only be used with objects or reference types.
